reforms.description: The government has launched an Employment Package in May 2008 that includes significant measures aimed at reducing labour tax wedges and promoting national employment services to enhance the skills of the labour force. Additionally, the Agency for the Development of Small-and-Medium Sized Enterprises (KOSGEB) will enhance its managerial and technical up-skilling programmes to support SMEs, subsidising up to 90% of eligible costs for training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills and reducing tax burdens can improve productivity and employment outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The Employment Package was launched and KOSGEB's programmes are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures are significant but do not represent a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: An Employment Package enacted in May 2008 entailed significant measures for reducing labour tax wedges and promoting national employment services emphasising the upgrading of the skills of the labour force.

reforms.description: The Turkish government enacted a Research and Development Law in March 2008, providing additional incentives for R&D, including tax deductions for R&D expenses and exemptions for R&D personnel.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives for R&D are expected to stimulate private sector investment in innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D Law was enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The enactment of a law represents a significant policy change in R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: A Research and Development Law was enacted in March 2008 providing additional incentives for R&D including the deduction of R&D expenses from the corporate income tax base.

reforms.description: Twelve Technology Development Zones (Technopoles) have been established around large universities, offering various tax exemptions and infrastructure support to promote R&D and software production.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of Technopoles is expected to enhance R&D capabilities and foster innovation through collaboration between universities and industry.
reforms.status_evidence: The Technopoles have been established and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of Technopoles represents a significant investment in research infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: Twelve Technology Development Zones (Technopoles) have been created around large universities.

reforms.description: The government plans to mobilise Organised Industrial Zones (OSBs) and Small Industrial Sites (KSSs) to support the growth of medium-sized enterprises and small firms by providing low-cost infrastructure and sector-specific services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing infrastructure and services to SMEs is expected to enhance their growth and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans for mobilising OSBs and KSSs are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to mobilise the Organised Industrial Zones (OSBs) and Small Industrial Sites (KSSs) more actively in the future.

reforms.description: The government aims to overcome the duality between formal and informal sectors by accelerating the shift of resources to the formal sector, which is essential for enhancing productivity and competitive performance. This includes a new investment package for the GAP region to accelerate public projects and a 'formalisation strategy' aimed at transitioning informal firms to the formal sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Shifting resources to the formal sector is expected to improve productivity and tax collection.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for formalisation and regional investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiatives are significant but do not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The package includes additional central government budget financing over the 2008-12 period so as to accelerate the completion of public projects and the development of the region.
